answer:
In slotted Aloha, a successful transmission occurs when exactly one station transmits. The probability of success is the sum over each station of the probability that it transmits while the others do not.  

\[
P_{\text{success}} = p_A(1-p_B)(1-p_C) + p_B(1-p_A)(1-p_C) + p_C(1-p_A)(1-p_B)
\]  
\[
= 0.2 \times 0.7 \times 0.6 + 0.3 \times 0.8 \times 0.6 + 0.4 \times 0.8 \times 0.7 = 0.084 + 0.144 + 0.224 = 0.452
\]  

Thus, the normalized throughput is 0.452.  

\boxed{F}
